codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly extends thread-patch-cache.ts to handle delivery-loop refetch target. The invalidateThreadPatchRefetchTargets() function now recognizes 'delivery-loop' as a valid refetch target and properly invalidates the delivery-loop status query using deliveryLoopStatusQueryKeys.detail(threadId). The implementation follows the existing pattern used for shell, chat, and diff refetch targets.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the existing pattern in invalidateThreadPatchRefetchTargets() for adding new refetch targets. The case statement for 'delivery-loop' mirrors the existing shell/chat/diff cases and correctly imports deliveryLoopStatusQueryKeys.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/www/src/queries/thread-patch-cache.ts:927-932 - case 'delivery-loop' follows identical pattern to case 'diff' with proper query key invalidation

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the skill procedure correctly. Phase 1 (cache invalidation) was completed as specified in the event-driven-delivery-loop-worker skill. Tests were added per the skill requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows tests added and all 29 tests pass, including 2 new tests specifically for delivery-loop refetch target

summary: Implementation is complete and correct. The feature adds 'delivery-loop' refetch target support to thread-patch-cache.ts by: (1) importing deliveryLoopStatusQueryKeys from delivery-loop-status-queries, (2) adding a case statement in invalidateThreadPatchRefetchTargets() that calls queryClient.invalidateQueries() with deliveryLoopStatusQueryKeys.detail(patch.threadId), (3) adding two unit tests verifying single and combined refetch target scenarios. All 29 tests pass. The implementation correctly works alongside existing refetch targets (shell, chat, diff) and follows the established patterns in the codebase.
